title: wasm: connect() closes old WS before new one authenticates — JS calls during async gap see stale closed client
description: wasm/src/api.rs NieClient::connect() closes the existing connection (line 162) before the new NieRelayClient::connect() future completes (line 165). During the async gap (WebSocket open + challenge/auth handshake, typically 100-500ms on a live relay), inner still holds Some(old_closed_client). Any JS code that calls get_client() during this gap (e.g., send_message() triggered by a setTimeout or Promise continuation) gets the old, closed WasmTransport and its send_with_str() call fails. The error propagates to the JS caller as a rejection, but there is no indication that a reconnect is in progress. Additionally, messages arriving from the relay during the gap (e.g., queued offline messages) are delivered to the old transport's onmessage handler, which invokes the old (no-op) notify_cb installed at line 83 — they are silently discarded.
